channel.title: Microsoft Agentic AI Weekly
channel.description: Microsoft-first weekly intelligence briefing for agentic AI implementation teams.

item.title: Microsoft Agentic AI Weekly - Week of 2 Mar 2026
item.description: AI Prompting Guide, Autonomous Agents in Action, and Ways to Build Agents: Nube to Pro â€” each offer practical context and implementation details.

item.title: Microsoft Agentic AI Weekly â€” Issue 2026-09
item.description: Single weekly digest focused on high-signal agentic AI updates for Microsoft builders.

item.title: Platform teams standardize agent delivery: reusable patterns, guardrail kits, and operating SLAs
item.description: Platform teams are publishing reusable guardrail templates, capability-restriction patterns, and SLA contracts to reduce per-agent reinvention.

item.title: Microsoft Agentic AI Weekly â€” Issue #002
item.description: The market is moving beyond demos into repeatable delivery.

item.title: Evaluation discipline hardens: red-team baselines, regression gates, and release confidence
item.description: Teams are shifting from reactive testing to proactive evaluation baselines, regression gates, and measurable release readiness criteria.

item.title: Microsoft Agentic AI Weekly â€” Issue #001
item.description: The Microsoft agent ecosystem is moving from experimentation to operations.

item.title: Enterprise agent handoff matures: runbooks, on-call design, and incident-ready copilots
item.description: Governance automation, observability baselines, and human escalation paths are becoming standard practices for enterprise agent deployment.

item.title: Governance gets practical: agent policy-as-code, observability baselines, and rollout controls
item.description: Enterprises codify governance into deployment/runtime checks; observability baselines are now minimum launch criteria. Safe teams combine platform controls with human escalation paths.

item.title: Retrieval quality becomes a board-level risk: data lineage, confidence scoring, and answer accountability
item.description: Leaders now ask for confidence traceability, not just usage growth. Source lineage and answer citation quality affect audit discussions.

item.title: From prototypes to managed agents: ALM templates, deployment rings, and runtime safety checks
item.description: Agent programs are graduating from pilot artifacts to governed release pipelines. Deployment rings and kill-switch patterns are now mandatory for enterprise confidence.
